title: haplophyllum consanguineum
intro: Haplophyllum consanguineum, also known as the Bitter Rue or Eastern Bitter Rue, is a perennial plant native to the Middle East and Mediterranean regions. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as an expectorant. Haplophyllum consanguineum can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as used in topical applications, but its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
